figures_detected.description: Engine bay view showing rocker cover area, referenced for removal step
Top view of cylinder head showing valves to be checked at No.1 cylinder TDC position
Cross-section diagram showing clearance measurement between valve lifter and camshaft
Top view of cylinder head showing valves to be checked after crankshaft turned 360 degrees
Close-up view showing notch rotation toward injection nozzle for shim removal during adjusting procedure

content_blocks.text: Check valve clearance while engine is cold and not running.

steps.text: Set No. 1 cylinder at TDC on its compression stroke.
notes: Install flywheel locking pin SST KV109B0112.
Check that valve lifters on No. 1 cylinder are loose and valve lifters on No. 4 are tight. If not, turn crankshaft one revolution (360°) and align as above.

steps.text: Check only those valves shown in the figure.
notes: Using a feeler gauge, measure clearance between valve lifter and camshaft.
Record any valve clearance measurements which are out of specification. They will be used later to determine the required replacement adjusting shim.

steps.text: Turn crankshaft one revolution (360°) and install flywheel locking pin SST KV109B0112.
Check those valves shown in the figure.
notes: Use the same procedure as mentioned in step 3.

steps.text: If all valve clearances are within specification, install the rocker cover. Refer to "CYLINDER HEAD", (EM-5027).

content_blocks.text: Adjust valve clearance while engine is cold.

steps.text: Turn crankshaft, to position cam lobe on camshaft of valve that must be adjusted upward.
notes: Rotate notch toward injection nozzle (see figure) to simplify shim removal later.

tools_referenced.name: Flywheel locking pin
tools_referenced.usage: Lock flywheel at TDC during valve clearance checking and adjustment
